main_poem: There once was a library so fine
With books that were dusty and truly sublime
Forgotten tales slept on silent shelves
And whispers of knowledge were quietly felt
And the library's heart was still truly mine
recall_poem: There once was a library so fine
With books that were dusty and truly sublime
Forgotten tales slept on silent shelves
And whispers of knowledge were quietly felt
And the library's heart was still truly mine

main_poem: An abandoned theater did stand in decline
Its stage empty, with silence so unkind
Where shadows of actors once danced themselves
And echoes of performances long since dealt
And the theater's grandeur was still on my mind
recall_poem: An abandoned theater did stand in decline
Its stage empty, with silence so unkind
Where shadows of actors once danced themselves
And echoes of performances long since dealt
And the theater's grandeur was still on my mind

main_poem: Midnight skies shone with stars so divine
The world slept below, in a peaceful rhyme
The moon glowed with gentle, soft wealth
And darkness was filled with a gentle melt
And the stars shone with beauty so truly divine
recall_poem: Midnight skies shone with stars so divine
The world slept below, in a peaceful rhyme
The moon glowed with gentle, soft wealth
And darkness was filled with a gentle melt
And the stars shone with beauty so truly divine
